name: Meriko
classification_notes: Meriko presents as a contemporary grotesque sans-serif with alternates for key characters and a range of widths from Condensed to standard, suggesting a rational yet refined grotesque lineage.
summary: A nine-weight grotesque from Juri Zæch spanning three widths with full italic coverage and variable font options across 56 styles.
description: Available in standard, SemiCondensed and Condensed widths, the family runs from Thin to Black with matching italics in each width variant. OpenType features include alternate forms for Q, a and y, tabular figures, slashed zero, fractions and case-sensitive forms. Variable font versions are offered for both upright and italic axes.
foundry_blurb: Meriko is offered across 56 styles starting from $40, with styles spanning multiple widths, weights and variable formats, alongside OpenType alternates and figure options.

name: Ralphi Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric typeface, but one that introduces warmth through exaggerated tails, finials and generously wide proportions, placing it squarely in the geometric sans category with humanist inflections.
summary: A geometric sans with deliberate warmth built in, designed specifically for screen interfaces and branding that wants personality alongside precision.
description: Ralphi Sans sits within the geometric sans genre but departs from its usual austerity through exaggerated tails, finials and generous proportions that give the face an informal, approachable quality. The design accounts for screen rendering at small sizes with deep joints that keep letterform connections open and crisp, complemented by automatic hinting. Six weights from Thin to ExtraBold, each with a matching italic, offer range across interface and branding contexts, and a variable font version is included.
foundry_blurb: Juri Zæch describes Ralphi Sans as a friendly geometric typeface built for interface design, one that introduces warmth and character to an otherwise austere genre. The design features deep joints to prevent clogging at low screen resolutions, exaggerated tails and finials for an informal tone, and generous proportions suited to screen and branding projects that want a clean look with some personality.

name: Picual
classification_notes: Picual is a sans-serif with opentype alternates suggesting humanist construction, offering optional double-storey 'a', traditional 'g' and 'k', and a serif 'I', pointing to a humanist sans with optional geometric or grotesk-leaning variants.
summary: A three-width humanist sans in four weights, with a variable version and a generous set of OpenType alternates for switching between grotesk and humanist letterforms.
description: Thirteen static styles span Regular, SemiCondensed and Condensed widths across Light to Bold, with a variable counterpart covering the full range. OpenType features include a double-storey 'a', traditional 'g' and 'k', a serif 'I' and an alternate ampersand, giving designers meaningful control over the typeface's personality. The condensed cuts and alternates make it a practical choice for packaging, editorial and branding work where space and tone both need managing.
foundry_blurb: Picual is available in 13 styles from Juri Zæch's foundry, starting at $40. The family spans three widths and four weights, with a variable font version included, and features opentype options such as a serif I, double-storey a, traditional g and k, an alternate ampersand, discretionary ligatures, circled numbers, fractions and case-sensitive forms.

name: Ralphi Round
classification_notes: Ralphi Round is a geometric sans-serif with rounded stroke terminals, sharing the same underlying geometry as Ralphi Sans but distinguished by its soft endings and characterful letterform details.
summary: A rounded geometric sans with a deliberate inversion: soft stroke terminals paired with sharply square tittles and punctuation, creating a structured friendliness across six weights with italics.
description: Derived from the same geometry as Ralphi Sans, Ralphi Round replaces straight terminals with rounded endings while keeping square dots and punctuation marks, a contrast that stops the design from reading as merely soft. Characterful letterforms on glyphs such as a, f, q, t, y and Q add personality, and OpenType alternates allow designers to shift the typographic tone between playful and restrained. The family covers over 200 Latin languages via Underware's Latin Plus character set and includes tabular figures, case-sensitive punctuation, fractions and circled numerals.
foundry_blurb: Juri Zæch describes Ralphi Round as the spirited counterpart to Ralphi Sans, sharing the same geometry and warmth but with rounded stroke endings for a softer, more tactile feel suited to brands and interfaces that prioritise friendliness and clarity. The defining detail is a deliberate inversion: where Ralphi Sans paired round dots with straight terminals, Ralphi Round flips the logic, using rounded terminals alongside sharply square tittles and punctuation to add structure to the softness. The family retains generous proportions, deep joints and playful character details, and comes with comprehensive OpenType features including multiple alternates for letters like a.

name: Lorne Variable
classification_notes: The family presents three distinct sub-styles — Sans, Flare and Serif — across shared weights, suggesting a humanist sans with flared and serif variants rather than a purely geometric or grotesque construction.
summary: A variable font family from Juri Zæch spanning sans, flared and serif sub-styles across six shared weights, with an array of OpenType alternates including Celtic caps and script ampersands.
description: Lorne Variable brings three related styles — Sans, Flare and Serif — into a single variable family of 19 styles, all sharing a common weight range from Light to Black. OpenType features include Celtic caps, classic O and Q forms, double-storey g, two ampersand variants, discretionary ligatures and fractions, giving designers substantial typographic control. The breadth of alternates and the three-way stylistic split make it suited to editorial and branding work where tonal range within a single family is useful.
foundry_blurb: Lorne Variable is offered by Juri Zæch as a 19-style variable font family starting from $40, comprising Sans, Flare and Serif sub-styles with a rich set of OpenType features including Celtic caps, alternate letterforms and script ampersands.
